summary: Store.ListIDs double-quotes the resourceType identifier with embedded-quote escaping.
reason: fmt.Sprintf("SELECT id FROM %s", resourceType) inlined the table name unquoted. Today's callers pass hardcoded values, but parameter placeholders don't work for SQL identifiers — double-quoting plus escaping embedded quotes is the SQLite-safe pattern and closes the injection class for future callers that might forward a --resources flag.
